masquers-sodality.name: The Masquers' Sodality
masquers-sodality.description: The master maskmakers of Venturia. Founded shortly after the Tragedy of Vallombrosa transformed the Autumn Masquerade into a cultural institution, the Sodality has spent nearly a thousand years perfecting the art of concealment. They understand something about identity that the rest of the city only partially grasps.
hierarchy: Master (by invitation only — access to the third floor)
masquers-sodality.known_secrets: Senior Masquers learn techniques journeymen are not taught — how to craft masks that project emotions, shift expression, or encourage specific reactions in observers. The third-floor workshop is not open to the public. The masters who go in are not the same people who come out.
masquers-sodality.masquerade_connection: Three Sodality members sit on the Festival Committee. The Master of Revels has been a senior Masquer for as long as anyone can remember. During Masquerade season, workshops run day and night. Some customers report dreams after wearing Sodality masks — dreams of attending festivals they don't remember.
masquers-sodality.disposition: Neutral, secretive, proud of their craft

veil-court.name: The Veil Court
veil-court.description: A semi-secret society dedicated to the art of masked performance. They know that in Venturia, wearing a mask and truly committing to a different identity makes reality briefly more cooperative. They have been documenting this phenomenon carefully. They have not shared what they've found.
veil-court.known_secrets: The Veil Court has empirical documentation of Venturia's supernatural strangeness — specifically the way committed masked performance bends reality. They have been studying this for generations. What conclusions they've reached and what they intend to do with that knowledge is unknown.

gondoliers-confraternity.name: The Gondoliers' Confraternity
gondoliers-confraternity.description: Every canal in Venturia falls under their jurisdiction. Their vessels are unmistakable: black hulls, bronze fittings, blue-tinged lanterns at the prow. The Confraternity predates the curse — their oldest records claim the guild was ferrying passengers when the di Errante estates still stood.
traditions: Silence under bridges — ferriers do not speak while passing beneath them
Payment placed in a small bronze dish at the prow, never handed directly
No passengers after midnight during the week before the Autumn Masquerade
hierarchy: Master (knows every waterway — including those that aren't supposed to exist)
gondoliers-confraternity.known_secrets: The Confraternity's oldest charts show things that no longer exist — and some things that shouldn't. Masters know the unofficial waterways. They have been navigating these canals since before the di Errante tragedy and know things about this city that no one else does.
gondoliers-confraternity.disposition: Insular, traditional, deeply knowledgeable about the city's waters

liminal-house.name: The Liminal House
liminal-house.description: An institution that has been caring for people found wandering out of the fog for centuries. They call them 'fog children.' They have records going back generations. They are not sharing everything those records contain, and some of the people who work there are beginning to notice a pattern.
liminal-house.policy: No questions about before. The past is past. What matters is building something going forward.
liminal-house.alumni_network: Former residents recognize each other by small signs — a particular way of folding napkins, certain phrases from old House songs, the habit of checking exits when entering a room. They help each other and keep each other's secrets.
liminal-house.known_secrets: The House's records of fog children going back four centuries represent the most comprehensive documentation of Vallombrosa's effects on human beings that exists anywhere. Some staff are beginning to notice patterns in the arrivals. The House is not sharing this information.
liminal-house.disposition: Benevolent on the surface, cautiously secretive about what they know

city-council.name: The City Council
city-council.description: Harbor lords, guild masters, and the temple authority — three factions that nominally govern Venturia together and actually war constantly. They all know something is wrong in Venturia. They disagree violently about what to do about it and who is responsible.
factions_within: Harbor Lords — control maritime trade, the docks, commercial interests
Guild Masters — control the Market Tiers, craft production, middle-city power
Temple Authority — religious institutions, moral authority, their own agendas
city-council.known_secrets: All three factions are aware of the escalating strangeness. None of them are handling it cleanly. Some are trying to suppress information. Others are trying to exploit it. The disagreements are both political and genuine.
city-council.disposition: Fractious, self-interested, officially responsible for the city's welfare

covenant-archive.name: The Covenant Archive
covenant-archive.description: A family-run institution that handles binding contracts and formal agreements. In a city where promises carry unusual weight, this is not a minor business. Their records contain agreements dating back centuries that some very powerful people would prefer stay buried.
covenant-archive.how_it_works: Citizens who want their promises to carry legal weight come here to have agreements recorded. The Archivists witness each signing with solemn ceremony, speaking words in an old language before applying their seals. For a higher fee, certain clauses make oath-breaking inadvisable. Nobody speaks openly about what happens to those who break Archived agreements.
covenant-archive.deep_archive: The oldest sections contain contracts written in languages no living person can read. Some of these documents predate the city itself. The Archivists maintain them with the same care as recent agreements — suggesting the obligations they record might still be active.
covenant-archive.known_secrets: The Archive holds agreements between parties that have been dead for centuries, contracts that seem to still be in effect, and records of transactions involving things that should not be transactable. Some very powerful people would prefer certain documents never be found.
covenant-archive.disposition: Formally neutral, deeply knowledgeable, quietly powerful
